description: Organization and per-user holiday dates. Used by the Sent Items Tracker flow to extend business day calculations past holidays, and by the Response Detection flow to suppress nudge delivery on holidays.

alternateKeys.purpose: Prevents duplicate holiday entries per user per date. Enables safe Upsert during bulk holiday imports. DateOnly (8 bytes) + Text(36) × 2 = 80 bytes, well within SQL Server's 900-byte index limit.

columns.description: The date of the holiday. DateOnly type (no time component). Part of composite alternate key. Flow 1 queries holidays in a 45-day window from the sent date to cover the maximum possible business day offset.
Name of the holiday (e.g., 'Independence Day', 'Christmas Day'). Primary column — used for display in Canvas App holiday calendar management screen.
The Azure AD object ID of the owning user (from Office 365 Users connector body/id). Part of composite alternate key. For org-wide holidays, this is the admin user who created the entry.
When true, this holiday applies to all users in the organization regardless of who created it. Flow 1 and Flow 2 query with: cr_owneruserid eq '{userId}' OR cr_isorgwide eq true.

notes.cardinality: Multiple rows per user. Org-wide holidays are shared via cr_isorgwide=true. A typical US deployment has ~11 org-wide holidays plus any personal days.
notes.business_day_calculation: Flow 1 queries holidays between sentDate and sentDate+45 days. For each weekday holiday in that window that falls on or before the computed follow-up date, the follow-up date is pushed forward by 1 day (skipping weekends). This is implemented as an Apply_to_each loop over the holiday result set.
notes.quiet_period: Flow 2 checks if today is a holiday before processing nudges. If today is a holiday and cr_skipholidaynudges=true on the user's NudgeConfiguration, the entire nudge run is skipped.
notes.bulk_import: The Canvas App provides pre-populated collections for common holiday sets (US Federal, UK Bank Holidays) that can be bulk-imported via Patch operations.
notes.security: UserOwned table with ownership-based RLS. Org-wide holidays are readable by all users but only editable by their creator.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically.
